en.commentary: Camp Chef finally has the trophy. The Gridiron 36 took the top spot in Smoked BBQ Source's 2026 head-to-head against Blackstone by 8.7 to 8.4, and after a Memorial Day weekend of testing burgers, smash tacos, and breakfast hash, I agree with that verdict. The built-in lid alone is worth the price gap over the base Blackstone, and the front grease bucket finally fixes the single biggest annoyance with rear-trap designs. Blackstone's Original 36" Omnivore still wins on dollars per square inch, and for first-time griddle buyers under $500 it remains my recommendation. Traeger's Flatrock 3 Zone is the wind-resistance king and the move for anyone in a coastal or hilltop yard. Weber Slate 36 hit the highest peak temperature in head-to-head testing at 708°F at 15 minutes, which matters if you smash burgers. Skip the Royal Gourmet unless you are buying your first griddle and want to spend under $300 to find out if you actually like flat-top cooking.

highlights.title: Camp Chef Gridiron 36 finally ends Blackstone's eight-year run
highlights.body: I have been recommending Blackstone since 2018 and the Gridiron 36 is the first competitor I would actually swap for. The hidden front grease bucket holds twice the volume of the Blackstone rear cup and pulls out for cleaning without bending over. The four independent burners modulate cleaner at low end, so eggs hold at 275°F without burning. Soft-close hinges on the built-in lid stop the cheap metallic clank that bothered me on every previous Camp Chef. At $799 versus the Blackstone Omnivore with hood at $497 you are paying for the lid plus a better grease system plus better fit and finish, and I think that gap is worth it for anyone griddling at least once a week.

highlights.title: Blackstone Original 36" Omnivore is still the smartest sub-$500 buy
highlights.body: The Original 36" Omnivore with hard cover lands at $497 with 768 square inches and 60,000 BTU, and nothing else under five hundred dollars touches that number. The Omnivore plate runs noticeably cooler at idle than the old standard Blackstone surface, which is what you want for breakfast service. Magnetic toolbar plus paper towel pivot plus folding shelves are the kind of small details that turn a griddle into a daily cooker rather than a Saturday novelty. First-time buyer who wants to test the flat-top life without spending Camp Chef money should buy this and never look back.

highlights.title: Traeger Flatrock 3 Zone is the windproof pick
highlights.body: FlameLock construction blocks wind better than every other griddle I have used in 2026 testing, and the recessed cooktop avoids the burner blowouts that ruin pancakes on breezy coastal mornings. TruZone heat insulation between the three cooking zones is real, not marketing, and the temperature differential between zones holds at 75°F or more even after twenty minutes. The five-year warranty is the strongest in the category by two years. The 594 square inch cooking area is meaningfully smaller than the 768 on the Blackstone Original, so households cooking for more than six people should reach for Camp Chef instead.

highlights.title: Weber Slate 36 wins peak temperature and rust resistance
highlights.body: The case-hardened carbon steel cooktop on the Slate 36 stays bonded against moisture better than any other surface I have tested, which matters in humid climates where Blackstone tops develop surface rust within a season. Peak surface temp hit 708°F at the 15-minute mark, the highest in Smoked BBQ Source's lineup, and that translates to genuine sear on smash burgers and seared tuna. At $999 it is the most expensive standard 36-inch option here, and that price is the reason it sits at fourth rather than first. Coastal homeowner with a humid yard who already trusts the Weber brand should pay the premium.

highlights.title: Royal Gourmet GB4000P is the only griddle to buy under $300
highlights.body: The GB4000P delivers 52,000 BTU across four burners with a 35-inch flat top and includes the hard cover for $299, which is roughly Pit Boss money minus the ceramic surface and durability budget. Nothing in this price bracket competes on raw specs. I would not recommend this as a forever griddle because the cart wobbles and the cooktop warps after two seasons of heavy use, but for the family who wants to test whether flat-top cooking is going to be a thing in their backyard, this is the lowest-risk way to find out.

zh-tw.commentary: 今年 Memorial Day 之前我把這幾台全擺出來連續測了一個禮拜，從早餐蛋餅、煎牛排、smash burger 到台式蚵仔煎都試了，Camp Chef Gridiron 36 是這次我最想換家裡那台的對象。內建蓋子加上前置式集油桶這兩個改動，直接把 Blackstone 的多年痛點全處理掉。Smoked BBQ Source 2026 年的對決也是 Gridiron 36 以 8.7 比 8.4 把 Blackstone 36 拉下來，這是 Blackstone 多年來第一次在單一媒體丟掉首選位置，我認同這個結果。話雖如此，Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore 在性價比這條軸上還是沒對手，預算一萬五以內想要 36 英寸大鐵板，買它就對了。住在風口或濱海陽台的話，Traeger Flatrock 3 Zone 的抗風能力真的有差，下沉式鐵板加 FlameLock 結構讓火不會吹歪。Weber Slate 36 衝最高溫的能力是測試組最強，15 分鐘到 708°F，做 smash burger 那種需要重火快煎的料理會明顯感覺得出來。預算只有萬元以下的話 Royal Gourmet 是唯一值得買的，當作鐵板燒入門機剛剛好。

highlights.title: Camp Chef Gridiron 36 結束 Blackstone 八年霸權
highlights.body: 我從 2018 年就一直推 Blackstone，Gridiron 36 是這幾年下來我第一台真心想換掉手上 Blackstone 的選手。藏在前方的集油桶容量是 Blackstone 後置式集油杯的兩倍，要清的時候直接抽出來不用彎腰繞到後面去拆。四個獨立燃燒區在低溫端的調節比較細，煎蛋穩在 275°F 不會燒焦這點對早餐很關鍵。內建蓋的緩衝鉸鏈關起來是輕輕一聲，過去 Camp Chef 那種金屬對撞的廉價感終於消失了。價差大概多 NT$9,000 換內建蓋、更好的集油系統、更精緻的做工，對一週至少開火一次的家庭來說很值得。

highlights.title: Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore 還是萬五內最聰明的選擇
highlights.body: Original 36 Omnivore 附硬蓋版定價 $497，換算下來 NT$15,500 有找，768 平方英寸加上 60,000 BTU 這個規格表，五百美元以下沒對手。Omnivore 鐵板的怠速溫度明顯比舊款 Blackstone 鐵板低，做早餐服務的時候這個差異感覺得出來。磁吸工具桿、可旋轉廚房紙巾架、可摺疊側架，這些小設計加起來讓鐵板從週末娛樂變成日常開火的器具。第一次想入手戶外鐵板燒、又不想直接砸 Camp Chef 那個預算的話，閉著眼睛買這台就對了。

highlights.title: Traeger Flatrock 3 Zone 是抗風首選
highlights.body: FlameLock 結構在 2026 年我測過的所有戶外鐵板中抗風能力最強，下沉式鐵板面避免了風大時的火焰外吹，沿海或頂樓陽台煎鬆餅的人能立刻感覺到差別。三個烹飪區之間的 TruZone 隔熱不是行銷話術，二十分鐘後不同區的溫差能穩穩維持在 75°F 以上。五年保固在這個類別領先第二名兩年，這個誠意要給。594 平方英寸的烹飪面積比 Blackstone Original 的 768 小不少，固定要餵超過六個人的家庭直接跳 Camp Chef。

highlights.title: Weber Slate 36 拿下最高溫與抗鏽兩項冠軍
highlights.body: Slate 36 的硬化碳鋼鐵板抗潮性真的有感，在台灣這種高濕度氣候下用 Blackstone 一季就會看到表面紅鏽，Weber 這個塗層硬度撐得住。15 分鐘衝到 708°F 是 Smoked BBQ Source 整個測試組裡最高的，做 smash burger 或炙燒鮪魚那種要重火快煎的料理，這個溫差會直接吃進味道裡。NT$31,000 的售價是這份榜單裡標準 36 英寸最貴的，價格就是它排第四而非第一的唯一原因。住在濱海或陽明山這種高濕度區、又信 Weber 品牌的話，多花這筆錢值得。

highlights.title: Royal Gourmet GB4000P 是萬元以下唯一推薦
highlights.body: GB4000P 用 NT$9,500 給你 52,000 BTU、四口燃燒、35 英寸鐵板加硬蓋，這個價位帶完全找不到對手。我不會推薦它當作長期使用的主力鐵板，推車骨架晃動感明顯、鐵板在重度使用兩季後會有變形跡象。當作測試自己到底會不會愛上戶外鐵板燒這件事的入門機，這台是風險最低的選擇。如果撐過兩年發現自己每週都在開火，那就升級 Blackstone Original 或 Camp Chef Gridiron 36，這時候你已經知道自己要什麼了。

en.commentary: Day 4 of Memorial Day week and the griddle leaderboard is locked. Camp Chef Gridiron 36 holds first at $799 across Camp Chef direct and Amazon, four sessions at the floor confirms the price is real heading into the long weekend. The fresh Thursday signal is that Blackstone cut the Original 36 Omnivore with Hard Cover to $447 at Walmart, fifty bucks under the Home Depot price I tracked Tuesday, which makes the value pick even more obvious for first-time buyers who want to test flat-top life under $500. Gridiron 36 stays first because the front grease bucket plus the built-in lid plus the four-burner heat modulation still wins the head-to-head on quality. Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore stays second on raw value. Traeger Flatrock 3 Zone holds third on wind resistance. Weber Slate 36 holds fourth on peak temperature. The practical advice today is the same as Tuesday, lock the Gridiron at the held floor or grab the Walmart-cut Blackstone Original if you want to spend half as much.

highlights.title: Traeger Flatrock 3 Zone is still the windproof call
highlights.body: FlameLock and the recessed cooktop hold up better than anything else in breezy coastal yards. Five-year warranty leads the category. Buy this if your patio gets gusts that blow out Blackstone pancakes.

Traeger Flatrock 3 Zone at third holds $899 with the $100 cut, the 3-zone heat plus the Traeger app integration plus the steel construction is the right pick for buyers locked into the Traeger ecosystem and the value math is locked at the premium price. 禮拜五早上，戶外鐵板燒這個品類因為 Blackstone 跟 Camp Chef 對烤肉季積極推價而開盤，跑出今年最深的國殤日週末折扣。

Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore Griddle with Hood 第二 NT$15,400 在 Walmart 國殤日砍 NT$3,100，36 吋鐵板加保溫罩加側邊架，是要 Blackstone 可靠度又不要 Camp Chef 價的買家對的選擇。Traeger Flatrock 3 Zone 第三守 NT$27,900 砍 NT$3,100，3 區火力加 Traeger 應用程式整合加鋼鐵結構，是被鎖在 Traeger 生態的買家對的選擇，價值算數鎖在高階價。

講真的，Blackstone XL 36-inch Original 第四 NT$12,300 砍 NT$3,100 當預算 Blackstone 選擇。Pit Boss Ultimate Plancha 5-Burner 第五 NT$17,000 砍 NT$3,100 當 Camp Chef 跨比較替代方案。

references.title: The 9 Best Outdoor Griddles for 2026 Expert Reviews

en.commentary:
Outdoor flat-top griddles have become one of the fastest-growing outdoor cooking categories over the past three years, and we are now at the peak of that growth curve with Father's Day driving the strongest purchase intent of the year. Camp Chef's Gridiron 36 holds rank one because it has solved the three problems that held the category back: the integrated lid enables true oven-style cooking and shields the surface from rain, the grease management system is the cleanest on this list with a dedicated catch system and easy-access drawer, and the build quality using heavy-gauge rolled steel delivers a surface that develops and maintains seasoning better than lighter competitors.

The Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore at rank two remains the most popular griddle in the country by unit sales, and for good reason. At 720 square inches and 60,000 BTUs with a lid and a value score of 9.6, it delivers the core griddle experience at a price that most buyers can justify without deliberation. The recent Omnivore version adds a more refined heat management system compared to earlier Blackstone models. Camp Chef's Gridiron earns the top spot on cooking experience, but the Blackstone wins on accessibility.

Traeger's Flatrock at rank three brings the brand's zone-cooking philosophy to flat-top gridding. The three-zone system with independent burner controls enables a level of simultaneous temperature management that is unique in the category. Traeger's build quality and the stainless steel side shelves justify the premium over Blackstone, but the higher price point means the Flatrock is a considered purchase rather than an impulse buy.

Father's Day promotions are running now: Blackstone is offering a free 30-piece tool kit with purchase, and Camp Chef has up to 200 dollars off select models at Sportsman's Warehouse. These deals are timed to the pre-June 21 buying window and worth acting on before they expire.

highlights.title: Camp Chef Gridiron Leads on Build and Grease Management
highlights.body: The integrated lid and dedicated grease management system are the two features that put the Camp Chef Gridiron ahead of the competition at rank one. The lid enables baking and steaming, cooking techniques that no open-surface griddle can replicate, and the grease drawer system is cleaner and easier than anything Blackstone offers at this price point.

highlights.title: Blackstone Omnivore Wins on Value and Popularity
highlights.body: The updated Omnivore model improves on earlier Blackstone designs with a more refined heat management system while keeping the price competitive. Its 9.6 value score is the highest on this list, and the free tool kit Father's Day promotion makes it the most compelling value proposition in the category right now.

highlights.title: Father's Day Promotions Are Live Now
highlights.body: Both Blackstone and Camp Chef are running active Father's Day promotions through June 21. Blackstone's free 30-piece tool kit and Camp Chef's up to 200-dollar discount at Sportsman's Warehouse are the standouts. These are time-limited offers that align with the peak buying window, act before inventory on popular configurations tightens.

zh-tw.commentary:
戶外平板鐵板燒烤盤（outdoor griddle）是過去三年成長最快的戶外料理品類之一，而現在六月父親節前夕正好是這個品類一年裡銷量最高的三週。

Camp Chef Gridiron 36 拿第一名靠三個別人做不到或做不好的優點：一是整合式鍋蓋，可以悶煮、蒸煮，甚至模擬烤箱效果，讓鐵板燒不再只能做高溫直火料理；二是油脂管理系統是清單裡面最乾淨的，專屬集油槽加上抽屜式設計，清潔比 Blackstone 方便很多；三是厚規格軋鋼板面開火處理後的蓄熱和養鍋效果是業界頂尖水準。

Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore 排第二是美國市場銷售量最高的戶外烤盤，靠的是 720 平方英寸烹飪面積、60,000 BTU 火力、附鍋蓋版本的 9.6 超高 CP 值評分。新版 Omnivore 款改進了熱場控制設計，比舊版更均勻。Camp Chef 在烹飪體驗上略勝一籌，但 Blackstone 在入手門檻和普及度上是市場之王。

Traeger Flatrock 三區獨立溫控的設計讓它可以同時在不同溫區料理不同食材，是這份清單獨一無二的功能。不鏽鋼側架和整體工藝品質確實比 Blackstone 高一個等級，但價格也明顯較高，適合認真投入戶外料理的買家。

父親節促銷現在就在跑：Blackstone 買就送三十件工具組，Camp Chef 在 Sportsman's Warehouse 有最高 NT$6,000 折扣。這些活動在六月二十一日父親節前截止，想買的話動作要快。

en.commentary: Griddles are the fastest-growing barbecue trend of 2026, and the season's reviews are crowning the machines I already have near the top of this list. The big story is the Weber Slate 36, repeatedly called the griddle to beat in the $799 to $1,049 range. Its rust-resistant, porcelain-enamelled cooktop is a genuine differentiator in humid conditions, it heats to 260C, and three independently controlled burners give you real multi-zone cooking. That is a serious flat-top, and it earns its rank four placement comfortably. So why do I still hold Camp Chef's Gridiron 36 at number one? Because when I weigh heat control, build quality, and grease management together, the Gridiron is the most complete cook I have used, and grease handling is where flat-tops live or die for me. The Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore stays at rank two as the value workhorse, the griddle I recommend to anyone who wants the most cooking surface per dollar and a massive accessory ecosystem. Traeger's Flatrock 3-Zone holds third on its excellent zoned heat control. The Weber Slate's porcelain enamel is a real selling point if you live somewhere humid and dread seasoning maintenance, and I would not blame anyone in the Gulf Coast or the Pacific Northwest for prioritizing it. I am keeping the order steady today because the Slate's strengths were already priced into my board. If you are buying for summer cookouts, the Gridiron is my no-regrets pick, and the Slate is the smart choice when rust resistance tops your list.

en.commentary:

For just a small step up in price over the standard Blackstone 36, you get a more refined daily-cook experience, which is exactly why reviewers have been flagging the Gridiron as the newest standout.

The Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore at rank 2 remains the people's champion and the best griddle for most buyers. The Traeger Flatrock 3-Zone at rank 3 is the premium engineering pick. The Weber Slate 36 at rank 4 brings a rust-resistant pre-seasoned top and Weber build quality, and the Blackstone Iron Forged 36 at rank 5 rounds out a strong field. Wind handling and low-temperature control are where cheaper griddles fall apart, and the Gridiron simply does not. The Traeger Flatrock 3-Zone holds third for buyers who want restaurant-grade zone control and are willing to pay for it. Further down, the Weber Slate and the second Blackstone keep their spots, and nothing this week reorders the list. My take is unchanged. Buy the Camp Chef if you want the best cooking control, grab the Blackstone Omnivore if you want the most griddle for the money, and step to the Traeger if precise multi-zone heat is the priority.

en.commentary: With summer cooking in full swing this first week of June 2026, the Blackstone 36-inch 4-burner remains the outdoor griddle I recommend to most people, and the season only sharpens the case. You get 720 square inches and 60,000 BTUs for around $400, which is enough room to run a full pancake breakfast on one half and smash burgers on the other. Testers came away genuinely impressed by how it handles both, and that all-in-one versatility is why a griddle can quietly replace several other cookers and save you both space and money. The Solo Stove Steelfire has earned best-overall honors elsewhere for its responsive tri-ply cooktop and rust-resistant design, so it stays high on my list as the premium pick for cooks who want faster heat response. Two honest trade-offs keep the Blackstone realistic: it struggled most in windy conditions during testing, and its lowest temperature runs hot at about 450 degrees, so delicate low-heat work takes more attention. For smaller spaces the 22-inch tabletop with hood is the one I point campers and tailgaters toward. This is peak griddle season, and while demand is high the lineup is stable, so I am holding the rankings steady this week.

en.commentary: Now that the summer cookout season is in full swing, I have had all of these fired up in the backyard, and the Camp Chef Gridiron 36 keeps earning its top spot. What sells me is the grease management, the smoothest path from cooktop to bucket I have used, which means cleanup after a big breakfast cook takes minutes instead of dread. The build feels like it will outlast the patio. The Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore stays my pick for the most people, simply because the value is unmatched: 720 square inches and four burners for around four hundred dollars, and a parts ecosystem you can find at any hardware store. It is the griddle I tell first-time buyers to get. The Traeger Flatrock 3-Zone earns its premium price through control. Its zone management is the most precise here, and in my windy backyard it held temperature better than anything else, which matches the wind-resistance testing I trust. If you cook in an exposed spot, this is worth the stretch. The Weber Slate 36 runs the hottest of the group, screaming past 700 degrees in about fifteen minutes, so for a hard sear on smash burgers it is a joy, though the price sits high for what you get. Down the value end, the Royal Gourmet GB4000P remains the honest budget entry: it cooks well enough for a casual weekend and costs a fraction of the leaders. My take this week is steady. Buy the Camp Chef if cleanup and longevity matter most, the Blackstone for the smartest money, and the Traeger if you fight the wind every cook.

en.commentary: I keep the Camp Chef Gridiron 36 at the top because the thing I value most in a griddle, edge to edge heat that stays put when the wind kicks up, is exactly where it wins. Fresh 2026 wind testing this week put a spotlight on how much breeze punishes open burner boxes, and the Gridiron's enclosed design and heavy plate held temperature better than the field. That matches what I see every cookout: fewer cold corners, faster recovery after I crowd the surface with smash burgers. The Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore stays my value pick at second. For the money you get a huge usable surface, honest BTUs, and a grease system that actually keeps the deck clean, which is the part most people underrate until cleanup night. Traeger's Flatrock 3-Zone earns third on heat control. Its zone burners let me run a screaming sear strip and a gentle warming lane at the same time, and that flexibility shows on brunch spreads. The Weber Slate sits fourth and it is genuinely good on flat ground, beautiful build and even heating, though the same wind testing reminded me it wants a sheltered patio to shine. Down the list the Pit Boss and Royal Gourmet keep their spots on raw value, they cook fine and cost little, I just expect more frequent reseasoning. Nothing this week justified a rank change, so I am holding the board steady and letting the grilling season data accumulate before I move anyone.

en.commentary: The Camp Chef Gridiron 36 stays at number one for another week, and the front grease trap is why I keep it there. Most Blackstones drain to the rear, which means reaching behind a hot griddle to empty the tray, while the Gridiron pulls grease forward where you can see and manage it. Add the soft-close lid, flamethrower ignition, and a pre-seasoned plate that needs almost no break-in, and it is the most complete flat top I have cooked on this year. The Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore holds a strong second on pure value: 720 square inches and 60,000 BTUs for around 400 dollars makes it the one I still recommend to most first-time buyers, and this week's roundups agreed it is the best griddle for most people. The Traeger Flatrock 3-Zone stays third for its precise zone control, the griddle for cooks who want to run a hot sear station and a low warming zone at once. I nudged the Weber Slate 36 value score up a tenth this week after seeing more reviews praise its pre-seasoned, rust-resistant carbon steel that genuinely cuts maintenance over years of use. That holds its fourth place. No new launch landed this week, so the rest of the board stays put. Buy the Camp Chef for features, the Blackstone for value, the Traeger for control.

en.commentary:
Peak grilling season is here and the Camp Chef Gridiron 36 holds my top spot because it is the griddle I trust to do everything well. The grease management is the best in the class, draining cleanly instead of pooling under the plate, the burners hold an even temperature across the whole surface, and the build feels like it will outlast a decade of summers. When one machine handles smash burgers, breakfast for eight, and a stir-fry without a weak spot, it earns the crown.

The Blackstone Original 36 Omnivore stays my value pick and it is the one I hand most first-time buyers. Around 720 square inches and 60,000 BTUs for roughly the price of a nice dinner out, it is unbeatable bang for the buck, and the enormous accessory ecosystem means you can build it out over time.

The standout in this week's testing was the Traeger Flatrock, which took first place in wind performance thanks to a cooktop sunk into the chamber that resists blowouts. If you cook on a breezy patio or lake dock, that design is a genuine advantage. The Weber Slate rounds out the top tier with excellent build and even heat, though it trails on wind. Buy the Camp Chef to do it all, the Blackstone to save money, and the Traeger if the wind is your enemy.

這週測試的亮點是 Traeger Flatrock，它在抗風測試拿下第一，靠的是把鐵板下沉進爐體的設計，有效防止被風吹熄。你如果在會起風的院子或湖邊碼頭開伙，這設計是實打實的優勢。Weber Slate 補上頂級組，做工好、受熱均勻，只是抗風差一截。想全能買 Camp Chef，想省錢買 Blackstone，跟風纏鬥就選 Traeger。

en.commentary: Grilling season is in full swing, and the Camp Chef Gridiron 36 stays my number one this week. It leads because it nails the details that make a griddle a joy to live with, tight even heat control across four zones, a thick well-seasoned plate, and the cleanest grease path I have used, which means less scrubbing after a big cook. The Blackstone Original 36 holds a strong second, and the market keeps proving why: at roughly 400 dollars for 720 square inches and 60,000 BTUs, it remains the best value flat-top most people will ever need, with a fan base so large the brand's hashtags have crossed a billion views. The Traeger Flatrock stays third on genuine engineering, with a recessed cooking surface that resists wind blowouts and U-shaped burners that push heat edge to edge, exactly what you want if you cook where the breeze is a problem. Reviewers this month keep drawing the same line I do, Traeger for wind performance and precision, Blackstone for value, and that balance is why my order holds. The Weber Slate stays my pick for build quality and a pre-seasoned surface that shortens the break-in, and the Traeger IronTop is the value entry into Traeger's ecosystem. Nothing this week reshuffles the list. If you are buying your first griddle, size the cooking area to your crowd, then choose based on whether wind or price is your bigger concern.
